item.title: 一键最大化您的数据收集效率！！ | Maximising your Data collection efficiency with One click!!
item.description:
在当前的数字时代，网络抓取已成为数据采集的重要组成部分。 由于在线可用数据量的不断增加，Web 抓取已成为从网站获取有见地数据的有效方法。 然而，网络抓取可能会花费大量时间，尤其是在处理庞大的数据集时。 我们将在本文中介绍如何通过单击来提高数据收集效率。

自动化数据收集过程

使用 BeautifulSoup、Scrapy 或 Selenium 等网络抓取工具自动化数据收集过程是提高数据收集效率的一种技术。 这些技术可以为从网站提取数据提供强大且适应性强的框架，从而节省大量时间和精力。

找到要从中收集数据的网站和页面是自动化数据收集过程的第一步。 找到目标网站后，您可以使用网络抓取工具自动从这些网站中提取数据。 这需要创建可以与目标网站通信并检索必要数据的 Python 或其他计算机语言脚本。

除了自动化数据收集过程之外，Web 抓取技术还可以包括其他功能，如数据清理、数据解析和数据可视化。 通过使用这些功能，您可以进一步简化数据收集过程并从数据中获取更有洞察力的信息。

使用 API 进行数据收集

利用网站提供的 API（应用程序编程接口）是另一种提高数据采集效率的技术。 开发人员可以通过为此目的创建的 API 从网站和应用程序访问特定数据。 无需手动从网站提取数据，您可以使用 API 以更有条理和有效的方式获取数据。

许多网站都提供 API，让用户可以直接提取数据。 例如， Twitter和Facebook等社交媒体网站为开发人员提供 API，使他们能够提取推文和帖子等数据。 通过使用这些 API，您可以更有效、更系统地收集数据。

最后，任何收集数据的组织都必须最大限度地提高数据收集效率。 通过使用网络抓取工具和 API 自动执行数据收集过程，您可以更有效地收集数据并从数据中获得有用的见解。 因此，立即开始调查网络抓取的潜力，只需单击一下，即可推进您的数据收集！



原文:

In the current digital era, web scraping has become a crucial component of data acquisition. Web scraping has become a potent method for obtaining insightful data from websites as a result of the expanding amount of data available online. Web scraping can, however, take a lot of time, especially when working with enormous datasets. We will go over how to increase your data-collecting efficiency with only one click in this article.

Automating the Process of Data Collection

Automating the data-collecting process with web scraping tools like BeautifulSoup, Scrapy, or Selenium is one technique to increase data-collection efficiency. These technologies can save a significant amount of time and effort by offering a robust and adaptable framework for data extraction from websites.

Finding the websites and pages from which you want to collect data is the first step in automating the data collection process. After locating the target websites, you may use web scraping tools to automatically extract the data from those sites. This entails creating Python or other computer language scripts that can communicate with the target websites and retrieve the necessary data.

Web scraping technologies can include further capabilities like data cleaning, data parsing, and data visualization in addition to automating the data collection process. You can further streamline your data collection process and gain more insightful information from your data by using these features.

Using APIs for Data Collection

Utilizing APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) offered by websites is another technique to increase the efficiency of your data acquisition. Developers can access specific data from websites and applications through APIs, which are created for this purpose. Without having to manually extract data from websites, you may acquire data in a more organized and effective manner by using APIs.

Many websites offer APIs that let users directly extract data. For instance, social media sites like Twitter and Facebook offer developers APIs that let them extract data like tweets and postings. You can gather data more efficiently and in a systematic manner by using these APIs.

In the last, Any organisation that collects data has to maximize data-collecting efficiency. You can gather data more effectively and gain useful insights from your data by automating the data-collecting process utilising web scraping tools and APIs. Web scraping and API integration can assist you in making better-informed judgments and gaining an advantage over your rivals, whether you are a data scientist, marketer, or business owner. Thus, start investigating web scraping's potential right away, and with just one click, advance your data collecting!
item.author: crawlmagic

item.title: 2023 年 10 大最佳游戏开发软件工具 | Top 10 Best Game Development Software Tools for Games in 2023
item.description:
游戏开发是一个价值数十亿美元的产业，随着移动游戏和游戏机游戏的兴起而不断发展。 游戏的成功不仅取决于它的概念，还取决于它的开发。 要创建成功的游戏，开发人员需要合适的软件工具来帮助他们设计、编程和测试他们的游戏。 在此博客中，我们将讨论 2023 年十大最佳游戏开发软件工具。

关于博彩业的统计数据

近年来，游戏行业经历了显着增长，其中手机游戏行业是增长最快的行业之一。 根据App Annie 的 2022 年移动游戏行业报告，移动游戏行业预计在 2021 年产生 1200 亿美元的收入，并有望在 2022 年达到 1580 亿美元。此外，视频游戏行业也在快速增长，预计将产生 230 美元的收入根据Statista 的数据，2022 年的收入将达到 10 亿美元。

游戏开发软件市场统计

游戏开发软件市场也在增长，根据 Grand View Research 的一份报告，全球游戏引擎市场规模预计到 2028 年将达到 37 亿美元。 该报告还指出，手机和主机游戏的日益普及正在推动游戏引擎市场的增长。 有许多可用的游戏开发软件工具，而游戏引擎是最受欢迎的。 在商业软件评论平台 G2 的一项调查中，Unity 是最受欢迎的游戏引擎，其次是 Unreal Engine 和 Godot Engine。

最佳游戏设计软件列表

它使用拖放式界面，使初学者无需编码即可轻松创建游戏。 它还支持 HTML5 游戏开发，可以轻松创建可在网络浏览器中玩的游戏。

它使用称为 Bolt 的可视化脚本系统，无需编码即可轻松创建游戏逻辑。 它还拥有庞大的开发人员社区和庞大的资产商店，开发人员可以在其中找到现成的资产和工具以用于他们的游戏。

它包括 3ds Max、Maya 和 Mudbox 等软件，

被游戏开发者广泛用于创作高质量的游戏美术。 它还拥有庞大的艺术家社区和庞大的 3D 资源库，开发人员可以在他们的游戏中使用这些资源。

它支持 2D 游戏开发，可用于创建适用于多个平台的游戏，包括 Windows、Mac、Linux、Android 和 iOS。 它还拥有一个庞大的开发者社区和一个开发者可以销售他们的游戏的市场。

它可用于为多个平台创建游戏，包括 Windows、Mac、Linux、

安卓和iOS。 它还支持 HTML5 游戏开发，可用于创建可在网络浏览器中玩的游戏。 它有一个庞大的开发者社区和一个开发者可以找到和销售游戏资产和模板的市场。

它使用一种简单的标记语言来创建可以在网络浏览器中玩的基于文本的游戏。 它是免费和开源的，拥有庞大的开发者社区，他们可以创建和共享游戏模板和资源。

它可用于为多个平台创建游戏，包括 Windows、Mac、Linux、Android 和 iOS。 它还拥有一个庞大的开发者社区和一个开发者可以找到和出售游戏资产和模板的市场。

它使用称为拖放的可视化脚本系统，无需编码即可轻松创建游戏逻辑。 它可用于为多个平台创建游戏，包括 Windows、Mac、Linux、Android 和 iOS。 它还拥有一个庞大的开发者社区和一个开发者可以找到和出售游戏资产和模板的市场。

它使用简单的拖放界面来创建可以在 Windows 和 Mac 上玩的游戏。 它还拥有一个庞大的开发者社区和一个开发者可以找到和出售游戏资产和模板的市场。

它可用于为 Web 浏览器创建游戏，并可集成到教育计划中以教授游戏开发。 它还拥有一个庞大的开发者社区和一个开发者可以找到和出售游戏资产和模板的市场。

它可用于为 Web 浏览器创建游戏，并可集成到教育计划中以教授游戏开发。 它还拥有一个庞大的开发者社区和一个开发者可以找到和出售游戏资产和模板的市场。

这些软件工具提供了一系列特性和功能来创建 2D 和 3D 游戏，以及交互式小说和基于文本的游戏。 它们还支持一系列平台，包括 Windows、Mac、Linux、Android 和 iOS。 其中一些工具是免费和开源的，而其他工具则需要订阅或一次性购买。 选择最适合您的需求和预算以及您在游戏开发方面的专业水平的工具非常重要。
阅读更多：如何从头开始创建应用程序

概括

近年来，游戏开发软件越来越容易获得，有一系列工具可以满足不同的需求和预算。 从免费的开源工具到功能强大的付费软件，总有一款适合各种专业水平的游戏开发软件。

对于初学者，GDevelop、Stencyl 和 Construct 2 等工具提供了简单的拖放界面，无需任何编码经验即可轻松创建 2D 游戏。 对于那些希望创建基于文本的游戏的人来说，Twine 是另一种选择。

对于更高级的游戏开发，Unity、Unreal Engine 和 Autodesk 提供了用于3D 游戏应用程序开发的强大工具。 这些工具由专业人士使用，并提供广泛的功能来构建身临其境且引人入胜的游戏。

无论选择何种软件，游戏开发人员都可以利用广泛的在线资源，从游戏资产和模板到在线社区和教程。

总体而言，游戏开发行业不断发展壮大，新工具和技术不断涌现。 有了合适的软件和资源，任何人都可以创造引人入胜、激动人心的游戏，供世界各地的玩家享用。



原文:

Game development is a multi-billion-dollar industry that continues to grow with the rise of mobile and console gaming. The success of a game depends not only on its concept but also on its development. To create a successful game, developers need the right software tools to help them design, program, and test their games. In this blog, we will discuss the top 10 best game development software tools in 2023.

Statistics About the Gaming Industry

The gaming industry has experienced significant growth in recent years, with the mobile gaming industry being one of the most rapidly growing sectors. According to App Annie's Mobile Gaming Industry Report 2022, the mobile gaming industry is projected to generate $120 billion in revenue in 2021 and is expected to reach $158 billion in 2022. Furthermore, the video game industry is also growing rapidly and is expected to generate $230 billion in revenue in 2022, according to Statista.

Statistics About the Game Development Software Market

The game development software market is also growing, with the global game engines market size expected to reach $3.7 billion by 2028, according to a report by Grand View Research. The report also states that the increasing popularity of mobile and console games is driving the growth of the game engines market. There are many game development software tools available, and game engines are among the most popular. In a survey conducted by G2, a business software review platform, Unity was the most popular game engine, followed by Unreal Engine and Godot Engine.

List of the Best Game Design Software

1.GDevelop

GDevelop is a free, open-source game development software that can be used to create games for multiple platforms, including Windows, Mac, Linux, Android, and iOS. It uses a drag-and-drop interface that makes it easy for beginners to create games without coding. It also supports HTML5 game development, making it easy to create games that can be played in web browsers.

2.Unity

Unity is a popular game development software that can be used to create 2D and 3D game application development for multiple platforms, including Windows, Mac, Linux, Android, iOS, and consoles. It uses a visual scripting system called Bolt, which makes it easy to create game logic without coding. It also has a large community of developers and a vast asset store where developers can find ready-made assets and tools to use in their games.

3.Autodesk

Autodesk is a suite of 3D design software that can be used to create game graphics, animations, and characters. It includes software such as 3ds Max, Maya, and Mudbox, which

are widely used by game developers to create high-quality game art. It also has a large community of artists and a vast library of 3D assets that developers can use in their games.

4.Stencyl

Stencyl is a game development software that uses a drag-and-drop interface to create games. It supports 2D game development and can be used to create games for multiple platforms, including Windows, Mac, Linux, Android, and iOS. It also has a large community of developers and a marketplace where developers can sell their games.

5.Construct 2

Construct 2 is a game development software that uses a drag-and-drop interface to create 2D games. It can be used to create games for multiple platforms, including Windows, Mac, Linux,

Android, and iOS. It also supports HTML5 game development and can be used to create games that can be played in web browsers. It has a large community of developers and a marketplace where developers can find and sell game assets and templates.

6.Twine

Twine is a game design software that can be used to create interactive fiction games. It uses a simple markup language to create text-based games that can be played in web browsers. It is free and open-source and has a large community of developers who create and share game templates and resources.

7.GameSalad

GameSalad is a game development software that uses a drag-and-drop interface to create 2D games. It can be used to create games for multiple platforms, including Windows, Mac, Linux, Android, and iOS. It also has a large community of developers and a marketplace where developers can find and sell game assets and templates.

8.GameMaker Studio 2

GameMaker Studio 2 is a game design software that can be used for 2D and 3D game application development. It uses a visual scripting system called Drag and Drop, which makes it easy to create game logic without coding. It can be used to create games for multiple platforms, including Windows, Mac, Linux, Android, and iOS. It also has a large community of developers and a marketplace where developers can find and sell game assets and templates.

9.RPG Maker

RPG Maker is a game development software that can be used to create role-playing games. It uses a simple drag-and-drop interface to create games that can be played on Windows and Mac. It also has a large community of developers and a marketplace where developers can find and sell game assets and templates.

10.GameFroot

GameFroot is a game development software that uses a drag-and-drop interface to create 2D games. It can be used to create games for web browsers and can be integrated into educational programs to teach game development. It also has a large community of developers and a marketplace where developers can find and sell game assets and templates.

11.Flowlab

Flowlab is a game development software that uses a drag-and-drop interface to create 2D games. It can be used to create games for web browsers and can be integrated into educational programs to teach game development. It also has a large community of developers and a marketplace where developers can find and sell game assets and templates.

These software tools offer a range of features and capabilities to create 2D and 3D games, as well as interactive fiction and text-based games. They also support a range of platforms, including Windows, Mac, Linux, Android, and iOS. Some of these tools are free and open-source, while others require a subscription or one-time purchase. It's important to choose the tool that best suits your needs and budget, as well as your level of expertise in game development.
Read more: How To Create An App From Scratch

Summary

Game development software has become increasingly accessible in recent years, with a range of tools available to suit different needs and budgets. From free, open-source tools to powerful paid software, there is a game development software for every level of expertise.

For beginners, tools like GDevelop, Stencyl, and Construct 2 offer a simple drag-and-drop interface, making it easy to create 2D games without any coding experience. Twine is another option for those looking to create text-based games.

For more advanced game development, Unity, Unreal Engine, and Autodesk offer powerful tools to for 3D game application development. These tools are used by professionals and offer a wide range of features for building immersive and engaging games.

Regardless of the software chosen, game developers can take advantage of a wide range of resources available online, from game assets and templates to online communities and tutorials.

Overall, the game development industry continues to grow and evolve, with new tools and technologies emerging all the time. With the right software and resources, anyone can create engaging and exciting games that can be enjoyed by players around the world.
item.author: emmajohns2023

item.title: Web 开发中的可访问性：为所有用户设计 | Accessibility in web development: designing for all users
item.description:
拉胡尔·索兰基

发表于 4 月 24 日

在本文中，我们将讨论可访问性在 Web 开发中的重要性，并提供一些为所有用户设计的最佳实践。

为什么可访问性很重要？

可访问性是必不可少的，因为它确保所有人，包括残疾人，都能平等地获得信息和技术。 互联网已成为日常生活的重要组成部分，每个人都可以访问它至关重要。

无法访问的网站可能会阻止用户访问基本信息、完成任务，甚至可能导致法律问题。

此外，可访问性不仅对残障人士很重要。 它还可以使有暂时性残疾的用户受益，例如手臂骨折、互联网连接速度慢的用户，甚至可能难以使用技术的老年用户。

除了确保所有用户都能访问在线内容的伦理和道德考虑之外，还有法律方面的考虑。 许多国家/地区都有法律法规要求网站可以访问，不遵守可能会导致法律诉讼和经济处罚。

总体而言，可访问性对于创建更具包容性的在线环境和确保所有人都能访问信息和技术至关重要。 对于 Web 开发人员来说，优先考虑可访问性并创建可供所有用户使用和访问的网站非常重要。

无障碍设计：最佳实践

使用语义 HTML

语义 HTML 是使用 HTML 元素准确描述它们包含的内容的做法，而不是仅仅使用通用的 div 和 span。 语义 HTML 提高了网站的可访问性、SEO 和可维护性。通过使用语义 HTML，屏幕阅读器和其他辅助技术可以更好地理解内容并为用户提供更易于访问的体验。

以下是如何使用语义 HTML 的示例：

假设您正在创建一个包含文章版块的网站。 过去，您可能使用通用的 div 来创建该部分，如下所示：

 <div class="articles"> <div class="article"> <h2>Title</h2> <p>Content</p> </div> <div class="article"> <h2>Title</h2> <p>Content</p> </div> </div>

但是，此代码不提供有关其包含内容的任何信息，这使得依赖辅助技术（例如屏幕阅读器）的用户难以访问它。

相反，使用语义 HTML，您可以使用 section 和 article 元素，如下所示：

 <section class="articles"> <article> <h2>Title</h2> <p>Content</p> </article> <article> <h2>Title</h2> <p>Content</p> </article> </section>

在此示例中，section 元素将内容的用途描述为文章集合，每个 article 元素描述一篇文章。 这提供了有关辅助技术内容的更多信息，并使残障用户更容易使用。

通过使用语义 HTML，您可以为网站内容提供更多上下文和意义，使其更易于访问、理解和维护。

为图像提供替代文本

图片是许多网站的重要组成部分，但它们可能会给有视觉障碍的用户带来障碍。 为图像提供替代文本（alt text）允许屏幕阅读器向用户描述图像。 以下是如何使用替代文本的示例：

 <img src="example.jpg" alt="A smiling woman holding a dog on a leash">

在此示例中，img 元素包含 alt 属性，该属性提供图像的描述。 替代文本“一个用皮带牵着狗的微笑女人”描述了图像的内容，并为无法看到图像的用户提供了上下文。
同样重要的是要注意，装饰性图像（例如背景图像或不向内容添加任何信息的图像）应该具有空的替代文本。

确保颜色对比

颜色对比度对于依赖高对比度来区分文本和背景颜色的视觉障碍用户来说非常重要。 确保文本与其背景具有高对比度可以帮助视力不佳或色盲的用户。 您可以在 mdn 网站上的以下文章中更详细地阅读此主题：

使用描述性链接文本

链接应该描述它们的目的地，并且应该在上下文之外有意义。 例如，“单击此处”不是描述性链接，而“阅读更多关于 Web 可访问性”是。

使用 ARIA（Accessible Rich Internet Applications）属性

使用 ARIA 属性可以使残障用户更容易访问 Web 内容。 您可以阅读以下文章以获取有关此主题的更多详细信息：

键盘辅助功能

有运动障碍或使用辅助技术的用户依靠键盘导航来浏览 Web 内容。 确保所有交互元素都可以通过键盘导航访问是 Web 可访问性的一个重要方面。
以下是改善网站键盘可访问性的一些提示：

  1. 使链接和按钮可通过键盘访问：确保仅使用键盘即可访问和激活链接和按钮，方法是使用 tabindex 属性和 Enter 键来激活它们。

  2. 确保可聚焦性和可见性：通过确保所有可聚焦元素（例如链接、按钮和表单字段）在视觉上与其他页面元素区分开来，确保键盘用户可以看到他们在页面上的位置。

  3. 提供清晰简洁的说明：为键盘用户提供关于如何导航网站和与网站交互的清晰简洁的说明。 这可能包括有关如何使用键盘快捷键或如何访问隐藏内容的说明。

结论：

无障碍设计意味着设计具有包容性且可供所有用户访问的网站。 通过实施上述最佳实践，Web 开发人员可以创建对所有用户而言更易于访问、使用和享受的网站，无论他们的能力或残疾如何。 通过优先考虑可访问性，Web 开发人员可以为每个人创造更公平的在线体验

原文:

Rahul Solanki

Posted on Apr 24

   

Web accessibility a topic that is often underrated especially among beginners and sometimes even the experienced developers, but what is web accessibility. Web accessibility basically means designing and developing websites to ensure that all users, including those with disabilities,like dyslexia or color blindness, can access and use them. In this article, we will discuss the importance of accessibility in web development and provide some best practices for designing for all users.

Why Is Accessibility Important?

Accessibility is essential because it ensures that all individuals, including those with disabilities, have equal access to information and technology. The internet has become a vital part of everyday life, and it is critical that everyone has access to it.

Web accessibility ensures that individuals with disabilities can use and navigate websites and online content as effectively as individuals without disabilities. Inaccessible websites can prevent users from accessing essential information, completing tasks, and can even lead to legal issues.

Moreover, accessibility is not just important for individuals with disabilities. It can also benefit users with temporary disabilities, such as a broken arm, users with slow internet connections, and even elderly users who may have difficulty with technology.

In addition to the ethical and moral considerations of ensuring that all users have access to online content, there are also legal considerations. Many countries have laws and regulations that require websites to be accessible, and failure to comply can result in legal action and financial penalties.

Overall, accessibility is crucial for creating a more inclusive online environment and ensuring that all individuals have access to information and technology. It is important for web developers to prioritize accessibility and create websites that are usable and accessible to all users.

Designing for Accessibility: Best Practices

Use Semantic HTML

Semantic HTML is the practice of using HTML elements that accurately describe the content they contain, rather than just using generic divs and spans. Semantic HTML improves the accessibility, SEO, and maintainability of a website.By using semantic HTML, screen readers and other assistive technologies can better understand the content and provide a more accessible experience for users.

Here's an example of how semantic HTML can be used:

Let's say you're creating a website that has a section for articles. In the past, you may have used a generic div to create the section, like this:

<div class="articles">
  <div class="article">
    <h2>Title</h2>
    <p>Content</p>
  </div>
  <div class="article">
    <h2>Title</h2>
    <p>Content</p>
  </div>
</div>

However, this code doesn't provide any information about the content it contains, making it less accessible to users who rely on assistive technology, such as screen readers.

Instead, using semantic HTML, you can use the section and article elements, like this:

<section class="articles">
  <article>
    <h2>Title</h2>
    <p>Content</p>
  </article>
  <article>
    <h2>Title</h2>
    <p>Content</p>
  </article>
</section>

In this example, the section element describes the purpose of the content as a collection of articles, and each article element describes a single article. This provides more information about the content to assistive technology and makes it more accessible to users with disabilities.

By using semantic HTML, you can provide more context and meaning to your website's content, making it more accessible, understandable, and maintainable.

Provide Alternative Text for Images

Images are an essential component of many websites, but they can present barriers for users with visual impairments. Providing alternative text (alt text) for images allows screen readers to describe the image to the user. Here's an example of how alt text can be used:

<img src="example.jpg" alt="A smiling woman holding a dog on a leash">

In this example, the img element includes the alt attribute, which provides a description of the image. The alt text, "A smiling woman holding a dog on a leash," describes the content of the image and provides context for users who are unable to see the image.
It's also important to note that decorative images, such as background images or images that don't add any information to the content, should have empty alt text.

Ensure Color Contrast

Color contrast is important for users with visual impairments who rely on high contrast to differentiate between text and background colors. Ensuring that text has a high contrast with its background can help users with low vision or color blindness. You can read this topic in more detail in the below article on mdn website:

developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Ac…

Use Descriptive Link Text

Links should be descriptive of their destination and should make sense out of context. For example, "Click Here" is not a descriptive link, but "Read More About Web Accessibility" is.

Use ARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications) Attributes

ARIA attributes are HTML attributes that provide additional information to assistive technologies about the purpose and function of UI elements. Using ARIA attributes can make web content more accessible for users with disabilities. You can read below article for more detail about this topic:

www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/ari…

Keyboard Accessibility

Users with motor impairments or who use assistive technologies rely on keyboard navigation to navigate web content. Ensuring that all interactive elements are accessible via keyboard navigation is a crucial aspect of web accessibility.
Here are some tips for improving keyboard accessibility on your website:

  1. Make links and buttons keyboard accessible: Ensure that links and buttons can be accessed and activated using only the keyboard, by using the tabindex attribute and the Enter key to activate them.

  2. Ensure focusability and visibility: Make sure that keyboard users can see where they are on the page by ensuring that all focusable elements, such as links, buttons, and form fields, are visually distinguishable from other page elements.

  3. Provide clear and concise instructions: Provide clear and concise instructions for keyboard users on how to navigate and interact with the website. This can include instructions on how to use keyboard shortcuts or how to access hidden content.

Conclusion:

Designing for accessibility means designing websites that are inclusive and accessible to all users. By implementing the best practices outlined above, web developers can create websites that are more accessible, usable, and enjoyable for all users, regardless of their abilities or disabilities. By prioritizing accessibility, web developers can create a more equitable online experience for everyone

item.title: Django 中的数据库 | Database in Django
item.description:
大家好，我是 Ankitha，在 Luxoft 担任初级软件工程师。 这是一篇关于 Django 数据库的文章，我最近开始使用它。

介绍：

它提供可扩展性、性能和灵活性，使其成为现代应用程序的热门选择。 在本文中，我们将探讨如何将 MongoDB 与流行的 Python Web 框架 Django 结合使用。

使用 Django 设置 MongoDB

在深入研究代码之前，我们需要使用 Django 设置 MongoDB。 我们将使用 djongo 库将 Django 与 MongoDB 连接起来。 以下是使用 Django 设置 MongoDB 的步骤：

在你的 Django 项目的 settings.py 文件中，添加如下配置：

 DATABASES = { 'default': { 'ENGINE': 'djongo', 'NAME': 'mydatabase', } }

在 Django 中创建 MongoDB 模型

现在我们已经使用 Django 设置了 MongoDB，我们可以创建一个使用 MongoDB 作为其数据库后端的模型。 这是一个存储书籍信息的示例模型：

 from djongo import models class Book(models.Model): title = models.CharField(max_length=100) author = models.CharField(max_length=100) description = models.TextField() def __str__(self): return self.title

在这个例子中，我们使用 djongo 库来定义我们的模型。 标题、作者和描述字段是使用 Django 的 CharField 和 TextField 模型定义的。

要指定此模型应使用 MongoDB 作为其数据库后端，我们只需从 djongo 导入模型而不是 django.db.models。 使用 Django 查询 MongoDB

现在我们已经创建了 MongoDB 模型，我们可以使用 Django 的内置 QuerySet API 查询数据库。 以下是如何查询我们的 Book 模型的一些示例：

 books = Book.objects.all() books = Book.objects.filter(author='John Doe') books = Book.objects.order_by('title') book = Book.objects.first() book = Book.objects.last()

在此示例中，我们使用与传统关系数据库相同的 QuerySet API。 其目标围绕提供一个可扩展、高性能和灵活的平台来存储和查询数据。 以下是 MongoDB 的一些主要目标：

可扩展性：

它允许用户轻松地跨多个服务器共享数据，以提高性能和分配工作负载。

表现：

它使用内存映射文件来提高读写性能，其索引功能允许快速查询大型数据集。

灵活性：

这使得它非常适合存储非结构化和半结构化数据，例如 JSON 文档，这些数据可以轻松修改和更新。

可用性：

它采用分布式架构，确保数据在多台服务器之间复制，因此即使一台服务器出现故障，系统也可以继续运行而不会出现任何停机。

使用方便：

它还提供了一组丰富的工具和实用程序来管理数据库，包括基于 Web 的管理界面、命令行工具和流行编程语言（如 Python、Java 和 Ruby）的驱动程序。

 import pymongo client = pymongo.MongoClient('mongodb://localhost:27017/') db = client['mydatabase'] mycollection = db['mycollection'] data = { 'name': 'John Doe', 'age': 30, 'address': { 'street': '123 Main St', 'city': 'Anytown', 'state': 'CA' } }

在此示例中，我们首先使用 pymongo 驱动程序连接到 MongoDB 服务器。 然后我们选择我们想要使用的数据库并创建一个名为 mycollection 的集合。

接下来，我们将要存储在数据库中的数据定义为 Python 字典。 在本例中，我们有一个包含姓名、年龄和地址字段的简单文档。

最后，我们使用 insert_one() 方法将数据插入到 mycollection 集合中。 这将使用我们定义的数据在集合中创建一个新文档。

请注意，pymongo 只是众多可用于 MongoDB 的驱动程序之一。 其他流行的驱动程序包括用于 Python 的官方 MongoDB 驱动程序，以及用于其他编程语言（如 Java、Node.js 和 Ruby）的驱动程序。

结论：

在本文中，我们探讨了如何使用 djongo 库将 MongoDB 与 Django 结合使用。 我们创建了一个存储书籍信息的模型，并演示了如何使用 Django 的内置 QuerySet API 查询数据库。 MongoDB offers scalability, performance, and flexibility that make it a popular choice for modern applications, and integrating it with Django is a straightforward process using djongo. MongoDB 提供可扩展性、性能和灵活性，使其成为现代应用程序的流行选择，并且使用 djongo 将其与 Django 集成是一个简单的过程。



原文:

Hi, I'm Ankitha working as junior software engineer at Luxoft. Here is an article on Django Database, which I've started using recently.

Introduction:

MongoDB is a popular NoSQL database that stores data in JSON-like documents with dynamic schema. It offers scalability, performance, and flexibility that make it a popular choice for modern applications. In this article, we will explore how to use MongoDB with Django, a popular Python web framework.

Setting up MongoDB with Django

Before we dive into the code, we need to set up MongoDB with Django. We will use the djongo library to connect Django with MongoDB. Here are the steps to set up MongoDB with Django:

In the settings.py file of your Django project, add the following configurations:

DATABASES = {
    'default': {
        'ENGINE': 'djongo',
        'NAME': 'mydatabase',
    }
}

Creating a MongoDB Model in Django

Now that we have set up MongoDB with Django, we can create a model that uses MongoDB as its database backend. Here is an example model that stores information about books:

from djongo import models

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    author = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    description = models.TextField()

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title

In this example, we use the djongo library to define our model. The title, author, and description fields are defined using Django's CharField and TextField models.

To specify that this model should use MongoDB as its database backend, we simply import models from djongo instead of django.db.models. The Book model will automatically create a collection in MongoDB with the name book.

Querying MongoDB with Django

Now that we have created our MongoDB model, we can query the database using Django's built-in QuerySet API. Here are some examples of how to query our Book model:

books = Book.objects.all()


books = Book.objects.filter(author='John Doe')

books = Book.objects.order_by('title')


book = Book.objects.first()


book = Book.objects.last()

In this example, we use the same QuerySet API that we would use with a traditional relational database. The all() method returns all books, the filter() method filters books by author, and the order_by() method orders books by title.

MongoDB is a popular NoSQL database management system that is designed to handle large amounts of unstructured data. Its objectives are centered around providing a scalable, high-performance, and flexible platform for storing and querying data. Here are some of the key objectives of MongoDB:

Scalability:

MongoDB is designed to scale horizontally, meaning it can handle large volumes of data by adding more servers to the database cluster. It allows users to easily shard data across multiple servers to improve performance and distribute the workload.

Performance:

MongoDB is optimized for high-speed data access and processing. It uses memory-mapped files to improve read and write performance, and its indexing capabilities allow for fast querying of large datasets.

Flexibility:

MongoDB is a schema-less database, which means that it does not require a predefined data model or structure. This makes it ideal for storing unstructured and semi-structured data, such as JSON documents, which can be easily modified and updated.

Availability:

MongoDB is designed to provide high availability and fault tolerance. It uses a distributed architecture that ensures that data is replicated across multiple servers, so even if one server fails, the system can continue to operate without any downtime.

Ease of use:

MongoDB is easy to use and administer, with a simple and intuitive API for accessing and manipulating data. It also provides a rich set of tools and utilities for managing the database, including a web-based admin interface, command-line tools, and drivers for popular programming languages like Python, Java, and Ruby.

import pymongo


client = pymongo.MongoClient('mongodb://localhost:27017/')


db = client['mydatabase']


mycollection = db['mycollection']



data = {
    'name': 'John Doe',
    'age': 30,
    'address': {
        'street': '123 Main St',
        'city': 'Anytown',
        'state': 'CA'
    }
}

mycollection.insert_one(data)

In this example, we first connect to the MongoDB server using the pymongo driver. We then select the database we want to use and create a collection named mycollection.

Next, we define the data we want to store in the database as a Python dictionary. In this case, we have a simple document with a name, age, and address field.

Finally, we insert the data into the mycollection collection using the insert_one() method. This will create a new document in the collection with the data we defined.

Note that pymongo is just one of many drivers available for working with MongoDB. Other popular drivers include the official MongoDB driver for Python, as well as drivers for other programming languages like Java, Node.js, and Ruby.

Conclusion:

In this article, we explored how to use MongoDB with Django using the djongo library. We created a model that stores information about books and demonstrated how to query the database using Django's built-in QuerySet API. MongoDB offers scalability, performance, and flexibility that make it a popular choice for modern applications, and integrating it with Django is a straightforward process using djongo.

item.title: 在 AWS 上构建无服务器应用程序 | Building a Serverless Application on AWS
item.description:
帕特鲁迪曼

发表于 4 月 24 日

无服务器计算彻底改变了我们构建和部署应用程序的方式。 借助无服务器，您可以构建可自动扩展的应用程序，只需为使用的资源付费，并减少运营开销。 在此博客中，我们将提供在 AWS 上构建无服务器应用程序的分步指南。

以下是您可以在此博客中学到的内容：

  1. 什么是无服务器计算以及为什么要使用它？
  2. 用于构建无服务器应用程序的关键 AWS 服务
  3. 使用 AWS Lambda、Amazon API Gateway 和 Amazon S3 构建无服务器应用程序
  4. 为您的无服务器应用程序配置安全、监控和测试
  5. 构建成功的无服务器应用程序的最佳实践和技巧 读完本博客后，您将更好地了解如何在 AWS 上构建无服务器应用程序，包括关键的 AWS 服务、实施和最佳实践。

第 1 步：什么是无服务器计算以及为什么使用它？

无服务器计算是一种云计算模型，其中云提供商管理基础架构并根据需求自动扩展资源。 使用无服务器，您不必担心手动配置和管理服务器或扩展资源。 这有助于减少运营开销和成本。

第 2 步：用于构建无服务器应用程序的关键 AWS 服务

第 3 步：使用 AWS Lambda、Amazon API Gateway 和 Amazon S3 构建无服务器应用程序

要在 AWS 上构建无服务器应用程序，您需要创建一个 AWS Lambda 函数，使用 Amazon API Gateway 定义一个 API，并将您的应用程序数据存储在 Amazon S3 中。 以下是这些步骤的高级概述：

创建 AWS Lambda 函数：您可以使用 AWS 管理控制台、AWS CLI 或 AWS 开发工具包创建 Lambda 函数。 您需要定义函数代码、执行环境和触发器。

使用 Amazon API Gateway 定义 API：您可以使用 Amazon API Gateway 控制台、AWS CLI 或 AWS 开发工具包创建 API。 您需要定义 API 资源、方法以及与 Lambda 函数的集成。

将应用程序数据存储在 Amazon S3 中：您可以将应用程序数据存储在 Amazon S3 存储桶中。 您可以使用 AWS 管理控制台、AWS CLI 或 AWS 开发工具包来上传、下载和管理您的数据。

第 4 步：为您的无服务器应用程序配置安全、监控和测试

为了确保无服务器应用程序的安全性、性能和可用性，您需要配置安全性、监控和测试。 以下是一些最佳实践：

使用 AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) 来管理对您的资源的访问。 使用 AWS CloudTrail 和 AWS CloudWatch 监控您的应用程序并检测问题。 使用 AWS X-Ray 跟踪和调试您的应用程序性能。

使用 AWS CodePipeline 和 AWS CodeBuild 自动化您的部署和测试过程。

第 5 步：构建成功的无服务器应用程序的最佳实践和技巧

以下是构建成功的无服务器应用程序的一些最佳实践和技巧：

使用微服务架构将您的应用程序分解为更小的、可管理的服务。 优化您的 Lambda 函数以获得性能和成本。

使用缓存和内容分发网络 (CDN) 来改进您的

原文:

patlu dhiman

Posted on Apr 24

   

"Building a Serverless Application on AWS: A Step-by-Step Guid"
Serverless computing has revolutionized the way we build and deploy applications. With serverless, you can build applications that scale automatically, pay only for what you use, and reduce operational overhead. In this blog, we'll provide a step-by-step guide to building a serverless application on AWS.

Here's what you can expect to learn in this blog:

  1. What is serverless computing and why use it?
  2. Key AWS services for building a serverless application
  3. Building a serverless application using AWS Lambda, Amazon API Gateway, and Amazon S3
  4. Configuring security, monitoring, and testing for your serverless application
  5. Best practices and tips for building a successful serverless application By the end of this blog, you'll have a better understanding of how to build a serverless application on AWS, including the key AWS services, implementation, and best practices.

Step 1: What is Serverless Computing and Why Use it?

Serverless computing is a cloud computing model where the cloud provider manages the infrastructure and automatically scales resources based on demand. With serverless, you don't have to worry about provisioning and managing servers or scaling resources manually. This can help reduce operational overhead and costs.

Step 2: Key AWS Services for Building a Serverless Application

AWS provides several services for building serverless applications, including AWS Lambda, Amazon API Gateway, and Amazon S3. AWS Lambda is a compute service that allows you to run code without provisioning or managing servers. Amazon API Gateway is a fully managed service that makes it easy to create, publish, maintain, and secure APIs. Amazon S3 is a highly scalable and durable object storage service.

Step 3: Building a Serverless Application Using AWS Lambda, Amazon API Gateway, and Amazon S3

To build a serverless application on AWS, you need to create an AWS Lambda function, define an API using Amazon API Gateway, and store your application data in Amazon S3. Here's a high-level overview of the steps:

Create an AWS Lambda function: You can create a Lambda function using the AWS Management Console, AWS CLI, or AWS SDKs. You need to define your function code, execution environment, and triggers.

Define an API using Amazon API Gateway: You can create an API using the Amazon API Gateway console, AWS CLI, or AWS SDKs. You need to define your API resources, methods, and integration with your Lambda function.

Store your application data in Amazon S3: You can store your application data in Amazon S3 buckets. You can use the AWS Management Console, AWS CLI, or AWS SDKs to upload, download, and manage your data.

Step 4: Configuring Security, Monitoring, and Testing for Your Serverless Application

To ensure the security, performance, and availability of your serverless application, you need to configure security, monitoring, and testing. Here are some best practices:

Use AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to manage access to your resources. Use AWS CloudTrail and AWS CloudWatch to monitor your application and detect issues. Use AWS X-Ray to trace and debug your application performance.

Use AWS CodePipeline and AWS CodeBuild to automate your deployment and testing processes.

Step 5: Best Practices and Tips for Building a Successful Serverless Application

Here are some best practices and tips for building a successful serverless application:

Use microservices architecture to break down your application into smaller, manageable services. Optimize your Lambda functions for performance and cost.

Use caching and content delivery networks (CDNs) to improve your

item.title: 如何使用 CSS Contain 属性优化浏览器 | How To Use CSS Contain Property To Optimize Browsers
item.description:
该库包含一长串函数和属性，可帮助我们在几行代码中开发功能，而不是编写混乱的 JS 代码。 这可能包括位置粘性、 媒体查询或设置网页的纵横比。

在我的 Web 开发经验中，我总是将 CSS 中可用的函数和属性分为两个部分。 其中一部分包含 Web 开发中与特定设计相关的简单项目，例如在用户滚动时将元素粘贴到顶部。 另一部分根据最新的Web发展趋势，对开发策略进行更高效、优化和改进。

今天，我们将讨论一个 CSS 属性，以及 CSS contain 属性如何帮助开发人员优化网页，让浏览器了解不同的元素以及它们之间的关系。

本Cypress 测试自动化教程将帮助您了解 Cypress 自动化的优势，以及如何安装 Cypress 并在线对多种浏览器和操作系统执行 Cypress 自动化测试。

重绘网页及其效果

要了解 CSS contain 属性，我们首先需要了解它解决的问题。

我们的浏览器采用 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 等神奇的原材料，并将它们转换为由数百名团队成员为最终用户创建的产品。 它应该完美地工作，浏览器与开发人员和测试人员一起承担着巨大的责任。 我们不能承受浏览器的任何错误。 让我们更深入地探讨一下浏览器的工作原理，以及当我们在提供 web URL 后按 ENTER 时所执行的过程。

  1. 我们的浏览器做的第一件事是创建文档对象模型和 CSS 对象模型。

  2. 创建渲染树以探索样式表中应用的样式并计算它们的关系。 例如，当我们使用CSS Selectors 时，我们定义了很多关系，例如 .my_class 或 div > p 等。在我们继续之前需要解决这些问题。

  3. 下一步是布局步骤。 在这里，我们计算在 DOM 树中定义的所有元素所需的空间。

  4. 完成空间计算后，我们就可以根据 JS 和 CSS 中给出的说明绘制像素。

  5. 最后一步是堆肥。 我们在上面的步骤中绘制的任何内容都来自多个图层，有时来自多个来源。 这必须通过仔细的布局计算在单个屏幕上进行整理。 由于一个元素可以与另一个元素重叠，从而产生视觉差异，因此合成步骤变得至关重要。

用图表总结它们看起来像这样：

为了提高网站的性能，开发人员通常会尝试优化这些步骤中的一个或多个，以便快速加载页面。 如果您的页面没有按时加载，您的跳出率会增加到 90%，而 53% 的用户会在三秒后退出。

本Cypress 自动化测试教程将帮助您了解 Cypress 自动化的优势，以及如何安装 Cypress 并在线对多种浏览器和操作系统执行 Cypress 自动化测试。

网页的绘制和重新绘制是连续发生的。 这取决于我们在做什么，我们看到什么，以及我们使用什么系统。

系统背后的逻辑很简单。 如果您正在查看 60fps 的图形，您的浏览器理想情况下（并且可能）在一秒钟内绘制和重新绘制 60 帧。 这是一个完全不同的故事，有它的优势。

另一件事是我们正在做的事情。 如果您正在阅读某些内容，那么在您滚动时，浏览器不需要重新绘制。 然而，滚动需要重新绘制网页。

最后一点，也是最重要的一点，就是我们的看法。 许多事情取决于您当前正在查看的网页及其各种元素。 如果网页有GIF，会不断重绘。 这同样适用于CSS 动画和其他CSS 过渡。

考虑到您正在阅读此博客的页面，Google Chrome 的开发人员工具可帮助我们了解每个网页的相同五个进程。 导航到检查元素部分，然后导航到性能选项卡。

单击 Record 以记录会话并重新加载页面。

您将得到类似于下图的结果：

如您所见，绘画比其他过程花费的时间少得多。 这是因为此页面上的大部分元素都是书面内容，很少有其他需要绘画的复杂内容。

让我们切换到 CSS 动画，您会发现许多有趣的动画可以在您的网页上实现。 重复相同的步骤来监控网页的性能。 这一次，有不同的结果：

注意：这只是我们模拟用户操作时获得的结果，即在具有典型图像的典型博客页面上，包括滚动。 如果你想看到激烈的绘画效果，你可以创建一个 GIF 并通过开发者工具观察性能。

这里的结果表明绘画占用了相当多的时间，这意味着如果我们可以减少它，我们可以获得更好的性能。

然而，即使我们将绘画视为一个单一的过程，它在数字媒体中也被分为两个逻辑部分。 在绘制像素之前，浏览器必须创建一个绘制调用列表。 这个过程称为光栅化。 当光栅化完成后，我们就可以根据开发者的指令来绘制每个像素了。 因此，在这篇关于 CSS 包含属性的博客中，考虑到我们在讨论绘画时隐含地包含了光栅化。

通过提及这一点，我确信浏览器引擎已经过高度优化，可以在必须呈现网页时发挥其作用。 问题在于开发人员如何设计网页。 如果浏览器认为页面上的所有元素都是新的，它将重新加载所有内容。

它分为多个部分，右侧和顶部将在您的章节加载中保持一致。 左侧导航栏随着高亮部分和中心部分的变化而变化。 如果我们保留重新加载整个页面的逻辑，我们就会不必要地绘制很多元素。

但是，如果我们专注于两个面板，我们可以减少绘制网页的时间。 这是我们在Web开发中面临的问题。 上面的页面很简单。 但是，我们的重新加载逻辑会极大地影响包含大量元素的复杂页面的加载速度。

在上线之前为 Safari 调试网站至关重要。 在本文中，我们将了解如何使用Safari 开发者工具调试网站。

什么是 CSS contain 属性？

它阻止了东西泄漏” ——Tab Atkins

上面的陈述可以用简单的词来重申和解释。 我们如何让浏览器知道各种元素的独立性，以便它在收到来自服务器的请求时知道哪些要绘制哪些不绘制？ 答案是CSS 包含属性。

所以如果我们想重新加载，浏览器可以省去重新绘制整个页面和重新计算布局的额外工作。 例如，具有许多如下小部件的网站：

这些元素是独立的，并在不同时间不断重新加载。 然而，请注意它们的布局，即它们的尺寸，将保持不变。

由于 contain 是一个 CSS 属性，它可以被赋值如下：

 element { contain: <value>; }

这取决于您在开发网页时分配给该属性的值。 尺寸

应用此元素的尺寸预计会在样式表中提及。 否则，它可能会折叠为 0。因此，浏览器只重绘元素而不是外部元素。

在第一个中，外部虚线框预计会根据内部元素调整大小：

但是当我们使用 contain: size增加元素的大小时，它应该保持相同的大小。

此属性通常不作为单一实现使用，而是与其他属性混合使用以获得更高的效率。

布局

为了清楚地了解布局属性，在定义它之前让我们看一个简单的演示。 由于布局属性很简单并且可以通过博客页面进行有效解释，因此我将用一篇文章构建一个：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en" dir="ltr"> <head> <meta charset="utf-8"> <title>The contain layout property</title> </head> <style> article { border: 1px solid black; contain: layout; } </style> <body> <article> <h2>This is blog one.</h2> <p>I am the content of blog 1.</p> <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="Hello I am a wooden box"> </article> </body> </html>

这将导致在 Google Chrome 上呈现以下内容：

这目前还好。 现在，让我们将以下样式元素添加到现有样式中：

这将呈现如下：

我省略了完整的屏幕视图以专注于图像及其位置，现在开箱即用。 由于没有使用其他安排来纠正浮动，因此它的行为符合预期并将图像移出。

检查 Web 元素以帮助开发人员和测试人员调试 UI 缺陷或修改 HTML 或 CSS 文件。 了解如何在 Mac Book 上检查。

让我们在其下方再添加一篇具有相同图片的文章。 于是我们的新代码就变成了下面这样：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en" dir="ltr"> <head> <meta charset="utf-8"> <title>The contain layout property</title> </head> <style> img { float: left; } article { border: 1px solid black; } </style> <body> <article> <h2>This is blog one.</h2> <p>I am the content of blog 1.</p> <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="Hello I am a wooden box"> </article> <article> <h2>This is blog two.</h2> <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="Hello I am also a wooden box"> <p>I am the content of blog 2.</p> </article> </body> </html>

这段代码的渲染如下：

由于我们使用 float: left 表示图像，下一篇文章就放在它旁边，因为空间可用。 框号 2 也发生了类似的事情。但是如果我们分析我们的代码，尤其是文章块，我们会看到图像是在这个块内编码的，但在浏览器上呈现时是在外部绘制的。

 <article> <h2>This is blog one.</h2> <p>I am the content of blog 1.</p> <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="Hello I am a wooden box"> </article>

这是因为浏览器无法分析出哪些东西是一个单元，哪些是不同的。 结果，我们在上面看到了这种错位，浏览器不得不重新绘制整个网页以呈现更改。 这两件事是我们通过 CSS contain 属性中的布局值来解决的。

布局值向浏览器指定我们将值附加到哪个元素完全独立于其外部元素。 通俗地说，我们在这个元素上做的任何改变都不应该反映到外面，我们在外面做的任何改变都不应该反映到这个元素上。 结果，重绘变得更少，因为浏览器知道如果对该元素进行了更改，则只有该元素需要重绘，反之亦然。

在我们上面的代码中，将以下属性添加到样式元素中的文章块：

所以，我们的样式块现在变成了：

 <style> img { float: left; } article { border: 1px solid black; contain: layout; } </style>

此更改会导致在浏览器上呈现以下内容：

这正是我们想要在网页上呈现的内容。 属于一个文章块的所有元素都包含在边界内。 在外部所做的任何更改都不会反映此处的其他文章块。 浮动也包含在里面，如上面的演示所示。

此外，边距不希望沿边界折叠，包含框创建堆叠上下文； 因此，可以使用 z-index。

画

我们可以在 CSS contain 属性中使用的下一个属性是 paint。 除了一小部分之外，此属性类似于上面讨论的布局属性。 如果我们将 contain: paint 应用于元素，它的工作方式类似于普通 HTML 中的 overflow: hidden 属性。 然而，这些特性仍然类似于布局值。

或者让溢出的元素在那里，但它们不应该在框外可见。

让我们分析上面使用的相同代码，除了两个小的变化。 这次我们将 contain 作为 paint，文章的高度为 150px，因此它比盒子的高度短，盒子是它的后代。

最终代码变成如下：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en" dir="ltr"> <head> <meta charset="utf-8"> <title>The contain paint property</title> <style> img { float: left; } article { border: 1px solid black; contain: paint; height: 150px; } </style> </head> <body> <article> <h2>Heading of a nice article</h2> <p>Content here.</p> <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="I just showed up"> </article> <article> <h2>Another heading of another article</h2> <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="placeholder"> <p>More content here.</p> </article> </body> </html>

最终渲染结果如下：

请注意，即使该框应该溢出（如果您删除 contain: paint 行，它就会溢出），但它不会由于 paint 值而溢出。 因此，在该元素内，即使用户不可见，所有内容都将保留在其中。

需要适用于 Windows 的 Safari 浏览器的出色解决方案？ 忘掉仿真器或模拟器吧——使用真正的在线浏览器。 免费试用 LambdaTest。

风格

样式值在使用 CSS contain 属性时并不常用，因为它的实现范围很窄。 然后规范通过开引号和闭引号属性声明了相同的效果。

该规范讨论了计数器和报价。 当我们必须对某些东西进行渐进编号时，CSS 中会使用计数器，例如在像带编号的项目符号列表这样的列表中。 这里我们使用 counter-reset 初始化计数器，定义值，并使用 counter-increment 在我们的代码中递增它。 这有助于我们跟踪计数器，类似于变量。

引号只是我们想要使用的符号。 例如，我可以在 CSS 中按如下方式定义引号：

 element { quotes: “<”, “>”; }

那么，“风格”是如何发挥作用的呢？ 当我们将 CSS 包含中的样式值用于任何元素时，我们将该元素与已经运行的计数器和引号的外部范围隔离开来。 这个元素将启动它的新计数器和报价，一旦我们关闭这个元素，外部代码就不会知道它的运行。 因此，在 CSS contain 属性中，具有样式的元素不知道运行在外部的计数器和引号，反之亦然。

让我们使用引用示例来分析相同的效果，如下所示。

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en" dir="ltr"> <head> <meta charset="utf-8"> <title>The contain style property</title> <style> body { quotes: "<<" ">>" "<" ">"; } .left-quote:before { content: open-quote; } .right-quote:after { content: close-quote; } </style> </head> <body> <center> <div> <span class="left-quote"> Hi <span style="contain: style;"> <span class="left-quote"> Bye </span> </span> </span> <span class="right-quote"></span> </div> </center> </body> </html>

在这里，由于在最后一个跨度中我们使用了类“right-quote”，我们期望它会关闭最后打开的引号“<”。 相反，它给出了以下渲染：

这表示外部跨度未被视为包含样式的内部跨度。

这个属性在实际实现中很少使用。

因此，我们必须实现这些值的组合，并在我们的网页上利用它们。 出于同样的原因，CSS containment 提供了两个速记值：

在这里，content 值是 layout 和 paint 的简写，而 strict 是 size、layout 和 paint 的简写。 如果需要的话，你可以直接在你的代码中使用这些速记。

在多个浏览器上测试你的 CSS 包含代码

规划您的网页设计以及哪些部分将附加到哪些 CSS 包含价值只是工作的一半。 想想如果一个浏览器没有在布局部分显示预期的结果会发生什么。 如果它显示元素边界外的框怎么办？

事实证明，此类错误对企业来说是毁灭性的。 最好的选择始终是确保您的网站在各种平台上兼容。 这可以使用在线跨浏览器测试平台（例如 LambdaTest）来实现。 它允许您执行Web 测试，并提供一个包含 3000 多种浏览器和操作系统组合的在线浏览器场。

订阅LambdaTest YouTube 频道以获取有关测试自动化、 Cypress 测试自动化、 Playwright 自动化等的更多教程。

第 1 步：要开始测试，如果您没有帐户，请在 LambdaTest 平台上注册。

第 2 步：从左侧工具栏导航到实时测试 > 浏览器测试面板。

第 3 步：输入您要检查 CSS 包含属性的网站 URL。 您可以在此处选择浏览器、版本、操作系统和分辨率。 以相同的规格启动网站。

第 4 步：按开始启动测试会话。

现在您可以像用户一样在浏览器和您选择的其他规格上浏览网站。 从测试人员的角度来看，它非常快速和方便。

需要在 Windows 上进行Safari 浏览器在线测试的出色解决方案吗？ 忘掉仿真器或模拟器吧——使用真正的在线浏览器。 免费试用 LambdaTest。

结论

更常见的是，开发人员面临与溢出、错位以及甚至感觉不合逻辑的事情相关的问题。 事情变得不合时宜，这很难解码，尤其是在从事大型项目时。 但是 CSS 包含的属性可以帮助我们展望未来，并以几乎 100% 的概率预测我们对元素的确切排列。

这会更快地加载页面，并且除了重新绘制之外，它确实可以帮助我们控制可能需要大量特殊安排的事情。 在实际情况下实施时，这些值会带来不同的东西，以适应不同的用例。 在这个独特的 CSS 包含属性的详细讨论结束时，请在评论部分告诉我们您的想法。 感谢您花宝贵的时间给这篇文章。



原文:

The integral part of web development is cascading style sheets, which are popularly called CSS. The library contains a long list of functions and properties that help us develop functionalities in a couple of lines rather than writing messy JS code. This may include position sticky, media queries, or setting up the aspect ratio for the web page.

In my web dev experience, I always divided the functions and properties available in CSS into two partitions. One section contains easy out specific design-related items in web development, such as sticking an element to the top while the user scrolls. The other section contains better efficiency, optimization, and improvement of the development strategies according to the latest web development trends.

Today, we will talk about a CSS property and how the CSS contain property helps developers optimize a web page and let browsers understand the different elements and their relations with each other.

This Cypress test automation tutorial will help you learn the benefits of Cypress automation, and how to install Cypress and execute Cypress automation testing over scores of browsers and operating systems online.

Repainting a web page and its effects

To understand the CSS contain property, we need first to understand the problem that it addresses.

Our browser takes magical raw materials like HTML, CSS, and JavaScript and converts them into a product created by hundreds of team members for the end user. It should work perfectly, and browsers have a huge responsibility, along with the developers and testers. We cannot afford a single mistake from our browser. Let’s explore a bit deeper into the working of a browser and the processes performed when we press ENTER after giving a web URL.

  1. The first thing our browser does is to create a document object model and a CSS object model.

  2. A render tree is created to explore the styles applied in the styling sheet and calculate their relations. For instance, when we use CSS Selectors, we define many relations such as .my_class or div > p, etc. This needs to be sorted out before we move ahead.

  3. The next step is the layout step. Here, we calculate the required space for all the elements we defined in our DOM tree.

  4. Once we are done with space calculation, we are ready to paint the pixels according to the instructions given in JS and CSS.

  5. The last step is composting. Whatever we drew in the step above comes from multiple layers and sometimes from multiple sources. This must be sorted out on a single screen with careful layout calculation. Since one element can overlap another, creating visual discrepancies, the composition step becomes crucial.

Summarizing them with a diagram would look like this:

To improve a website’s performance, developers often try to optimize one or more of these steps so that the page loads quickly. If your page does not load on time, your bounce rate increases to 90%, while 53% of users drop out after three seconds.

This Cypress automation testing tutorial will help you learn the benefits of Cypress automation, and how to install Cypress and execute Cypress automation testing over scores of browsers and operating systems online.

Painting and repainting of a web page happen continuously. It depends on what we are doing, what we view, and what system we use.

The logic behind the system is simple. If you are viewing a 60fps graphic, your browser is ideally (and probably) painting and repainting 60 frames in one second. This is a different story altogether, with its advantages.

Another thing is what we are doing. If you are reading something, then by the time you scroll, the browser need not repaint. Scrolling, however, needs repainting the web page.

The final point of concern, the most important, is what we view. Many things depend upon the web page you are currently viewing and its various elements. If the web page has a GIF, it will be repainted continuously. The same goes for CSS animations and other CSS transitions.

Considering this page on which you are reading this blog, Google Chrome’s developer tools help us understand the same five processes for each web page. Navigate to the inspect elements sections and then to the Performance tab.

Click Record to record the session and reload the page.

You will get a result similar to the below image:

As you see, painting takes considerably less time than the other processes. That’s because most of the elements on this page are written content with very few other complications that require painting.

Let’s switch to CSS animations where you will find many interesting animations to implement on your web page. Repeat the same steps to monitor the performance of the web page. This time, there are different results:

Note: This is just the result we achieve when we mimic the user’s action, i.e., on a typical blog page with typical images, including scrolling. If you want to see drastic painting results, you can create a GIF and observe the performance through developer tools.

The result here indicates that a considerable amount of time is taken by painting, which means if we can reduce it, we can achieve better performance.

However, even if we talk about painting as a single process, it is divided into two logical sections in digital mediums. Before painting the pixels, the browser must create a list of draw calls. This process is termed rasterization. When rasterization is done, we can paint each pixel according to the developer’s instructions. So in this blog on CSS containing property, consider that we implicitly include rasterization when discussing painting.

By mentioning this point, I am certain that browser engines are highly optimized to do their part when a web page has to be rendered. The problem comes with how a developer designs a web page. If a browser thinks that all the elements on the page are new, it will reload everything.

It is divided into various sections, from which the right and top would remain consistent on your chapter load. The left navigation bar changes with the highlight and center parts change. If we keep the logic to reload the complete page, we are unnecessarily painting a lot of elements.

However, if we focus on two panels, we can reduce the time to paint the web page. This is the problem that we face in web development. The above page is quite simple. However, our reloading logic can drastically affect the loading speed for complex pages with lots of elements.

It’s crucial to debug websites for Safari before pushing them live. In this article, we look at how to debug websites using Developer tools for Safari.

What is CSS contain property?

“CSS contain property doesn’t block things going into the subtree; it blocks things leaking out” — Tab Atkins

The above statement can be reiterated and paraphrased in simple words. How can we let the browser know about the independence of various elements so that it knows which ones to paint and which not to when a request is received from the server? The answer to this is the CSS contain property.

The CSS contain property can hint to the browser about one element not related to another element. So if we want to reload, the browser can spare the extra work of re-painting the complete page and re-calculating the layout. For example, websites with many widgets like the below:

These elements are independent and constantly reloaded at various times. However, note that their layout, i.e., their dimensions, would remain the same.

Syntax for CSS contain

Since contain is a CSS property, it can be assigned a value as follows:

element {
contain: <value>;
}

The < value > can hold any one of the supported values, which are discussed in the next section.

Values supported by CSS contain property

The behavior of the CSS contain property is not generalized. It depends on the value you assign this property while developing the web pages. CSS containment supports the following properties:

Size

The size value of the CSS contain property resizes the associated element without affecting its descendants or their size. The dimensions of the element on which this is applied are expected to be mentioned in the styling sheet. Else, it may collapse to 0. The browser, therefore, repaints just the element and not the outer elements.

In the first one, the outer dotted box is expected to resize according to the element inside:

But it is expected to remain the same size when we increase the element’s size with contain:size.

This property is generally not used as a single implementation but mixed with others to gain more efficiency.

Layout

The layout value of CSS contain property is a popular choice for developers because of the behavior change it introduces to the elements and how the browser perceives them.

To clearly understand the layout property, let’s see a simple demonstration before we define it. Since layout property is easy and can be effectively explained by a blog page, I will construct one with a single article:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" dir="ltr">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>The contain layout property</title>
</head>
<style>
    article {
      border: 1px solid black;
      contain: layout;
    }
</style>
<body>
<article>
      <h2>This is blog one.</h2>
      <p>I am the content of blog 1.</p>
      <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="Hello I am a wooden box">
</article>
</body>
</html>

This will result in the following render on Google Chrome:

This is alright as of now. Now, let’s add the following style element to the existing style:

This will render as follows:

I have omitted the complete screen view to focus on the image and its location, which is now out of the box. Since no other arrangements are used to rectify the floats, it behaves as expected and moves the image out.

Inspect web elements to help developers and testers to debug UI flaws or make modifications in HTML or CSS files. Learn how to inspect on MacBook.

Let’s add one more article with the same image just below it. So our new code becomes as follows:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" dir="ltr">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>The contain layout property</title>
</head>
<style>
    img {
        float: left;
        }
    article {
      border: 1px solid black;
    }
</style>

<body>
<article>
      <h2>This is blog one.</h2>
      <p>I am the content of blog 1.</p>
      <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="Hello I am a wooden box">
</article>

<article>
      <h2>This is blog two.</h2>
      <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="Hello I am also a wooden box">
      <p>I am the content of blog 2.</p>
</article>
</body>
</html>

The render of this code is as follows:

Since we used the float: left for images, the next article is placed just beside it as the space was available. A similar thing happened with box number 2. But if we analyze our code, especially the article block, we will see that the images are coded inside this block but are painted outside when rendered on the browser.

<article>
      <h2>This is blog one.</h2>
      <p>I am the content of blog 1.</p>
      <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="Hello I am a wooden box">
</article>

This is because the browser cannot analyze which things are one unit and which ones are different. As a result, we see this misalignment above, and the browser had to repaint the complete web page to render the changes. These two things are what we address through layout values in CSS contain property.

The layout value specifies to the browser that to which element we are attaching the value is completely independent of its outer element. In lay terms, whatever changes we do in this element should not reflect outside, and whatever changes we do outside should not reflect in this element. As a result, the repainting becomes less as the browser knows that if changes are done in this element, only this element needs repainting and vice versa.

In our code above, add the following property to the article block in the style element:

So, our style block now becomes:

<style>
    img {
        float: left;
        }
    article {
      border: 1px solid black;
      contain: layout;
    }
</style>

This change results in the following render on the browser:

This is exactly what we wanted to render on the web page. All the elements belonging to one article block are contained within the boundary. Any changes done outside would not reflect the other article block here. The floats are also contained inside, as seen in the demonstration above.

In addition, the margins are not expected to collapse along the boundary, and the containing box creates a stacking context; therefore, the z-index can be used.

Paint

The next property we can use in the CSS contain property is the paint. This property is similar to the layout property discussed above except for one small part. If we apply the contain: paint to the element, it works similarly to the overflow: hidden property in normal HTML. The characteristics, however, remain similar to the layout value.

The paint property instructs the browser that if any descendant of this element (the one with the paint property applied) is going out of bounds, clip it and present it within the boundaries. Or let overflowing elements be there, but they should not be visible outside the box.

Let’s analyze the same code we used above, except for two small changes. This time we take contain as paint and the height of the article as 150px so that it is shorter than the height of the box, which is its descendent.

The final code becomes as follows:

 <!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" dir="ltr">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>The contain paint property</title>
    <style>
        img {
          float: left;
        }

        article {
          border: 1px solid black;
          contain: paint;
          height: 150px;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
<article>
      <h2>Heading of a nice article</h2>
      <p>Content here.</p>
      <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="I just showed up">
</article>
<article>
      <h2>Another heading of another article</h2>
      <img src="wooden_box.png" alt="placeholder">
      <p>More content here.</p>
</article>
</body>
</html>

The final render looks as follows:

Notice that even though the box should overflow (and it will if you remove the contain: paint line), it does not due to the paint value. So, inside this element, everything will remain inside even if it is not visible to the user.

Need a great solution for Safari browser for Windows? Forget about emulators or simulators — use real online browsers. Try LambdaTest for free.

Style

The final property presented in the CSS contain property is style. The style value is not used popularly when working with the CSS contain property because of its narrow spectrum of implementation. “Style” works with only quotes and counters in CSS.

The CSS containment module level 2 states that when we use this value with counters and quotes, these properties must be scoped to the sub-tree and eventually create a new counter. Then the specification states the same effect through the open-quote and close-quote properties.

The specification talks about the counters and quotes. The counters are used in CSS when we have to progressively number something, such as in lists like a numbered bullet list. Here we initialize the counter using counter-reset, define the value, and use counter-increment to increment it in our code. This helps us keep track of the counter, similar to a variable.

The quotes are simply the symbols that we would like to use. For example, I can define my quotes as follows in the CSS:

element {
   quotes: “<”, “>”;
}

So, how does “style” play a role in this? When we use the style value in CSS containment to any element, we isolate this element from the outer scope of counters and quotes already running. This element will start its new counter and quotes, and once we close this element, the outer code won’t know about its run. So, in CSS contain property, the element with style does not know about the counters and quotes running outside and vice versa.

Let’s analyze the same effect using the quote example as follows.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" dir="ltr">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>The contain style property</title>
    <style>
        body {
          quotes: "<<" ">>" "<" ">";
        }
        .left-quote:before {
          content: open-quote;
        }
        .right-quote:after {
          content: close-quote;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
  <center>
      <div>
          <span class="left-quote">
              Hi
              <span style="contain: style;">
                  <span class="left-quote">
                        Bye
                  </span>
              </span>
          </span>
          <span class="right-quote"></span>
      </div>
    </center>
</body>
</html>

Here, since in the last span we are using the class “right-quote”, we expect that it will close the last opened quote which is “<”. Instead, it gives the following render:

This denotes that the outer span has not been considered the inner one with contain as style.

This property is rarely used in practical implementation.

Shorthand values supported by CSS contain property

The above values of CSS contain properties that help us optimize our website in a single dimension. So, we must implement a combination of these values and take advantage of them on our web page. For the same reasons, CSS containment provides two shorthand values:

Here, the content value is shorthand for layout and paint together, while strict is shorthand for size, layout, and paint. If the need arises, you can directly use these shorthands in your code if the need arises.

Testing your CSS contain code on multiple browsers

Planning your web design and what parts would be attached to which CSS contain value is only half the job. Think about what would happen if one browser is not showing the expected results in the layout section. What if it shows the boxes outside the element boundary?

Such kinds of mistakes can prove to be devastating for the business. The best option is always to ensure that your website is compatible across various platforms. This can be achieved using an online cross browser testing platform such as LambdaTest. It allows you to perform web testing and provides an online browser farm of more than 3000+ browsers and OS combinations.

Subscribe to the LambdaTest YouTube Channel to get more tutorials on test automation, Cypress test automation, Playwright automation, and more.

Step 1: To start testing, sign up on the LambdaTest platform if you don’t have an account.

Step 2: Navigate to Real Time Testing > Browser Testing panel from the left toolbar.

Step 3: Enter the website URL on which you wish to check the CSS contain property. You can choose the browser, VERSION, OS, and RESOLUTION here. Launch the website on the same specification.

Step 4: Press START to launch the test session.

Now you can surf through the website just like a user on the browser and other specifications of your choice. It is extremely fast and convenient from the tester’s perspective.

Need a great solution for Safari browser online testing on Windows? Forget about emulators or simulators — use real online browsers. Try LambdaTest for free.

Conclusion

More often, developers face problems related to overflows, misalignments, and things that do not even feel logical. Things go out of place, which is hard to decode, especially when working on a big project. But CSS contain property help us look into the future and predict our exact arrangement of elements with almost 100% probability.

CSS contain property help us optimize the renders and let the browser know which things to repaint and which not to. This loads pages faster, and apart from repainting, it does help us control things that could have required heavy special arrangements. CSS contain property accepts four values for implementation — size, layout, paint, and style.

These values bring something different that fits different use cases when implemented in practical situations. By the end of this detailed discussion on this unique CSS contain property, let us know your thoughts in the comment section. Thank you for giving this post your valuable time.
item.author: harishrajora12

item.title: 技术如何改变我们与狗的关系 | How Technology is Transforming Our Relationship with Dogs
item.description:
对于我们中的许多人来说，我们的狗不仅仅是宠物 - 它们是我们家庭中心爱的成员。 随着技术的进步，我们正在寻找新的创新方式与他们互动。 从可穿戴技术到互动玩具，技术正在改变我们照顾和了解我们毛茸茸的朋友的方式。 在本文中，我们将探索技术改变我们与狗的关系的各种方式。

智能项圈

智能项圈是最受欢迎的狗科技产品之一。 这些项圈可以追踪您爱犬的活动水平、位置，甚至它们的心率。 一些智能项圈还可以监测您的狗的行为，例如吠叫、咀嚼和抓挠，并在有任何变化时提醒您。

智能项圈的一个例子是 Whistle GPS 宠物追踪器。 这款项圈不仅可以跟踪您爱犬的位置，还可以监控它们的活动水平，并提供有关它们健康状况的实时更新。 另一个值得一提的智能项圈是Link AKC Smart Collar。 这款项圈不仅可以跟踪您爱犬的位置和活动水平，而且还有一个温度传感器，可以在您的爱犬过热或过冷时提醒您。 互动玩具

互动玩具是技术改变我们与狗互动方式的另一种方式。 这些玩具旨在刺激您的爱犬的思维，让它们长时间保持愉悦。

另一种流行的互动玩具是 iFetch Interactive Ball Launcher。 这款玩具旨在让您的爱犬连续玩几个小时，以不同的距离和角度发射网球。 通讯设备

通信设备是技术改变我们与狗互动方式的另一种方式。 这些设备使我们能够以我们从未想过的方式与我们的狗交流。

该设备是一种视频会议系统，可让您从世界任何地方看到您的狗并与之交谈。 另一种通信设备是 Furbo Dog Camera。 这款相机让您可以通过智能手机看到您的狗并与它交谈，它还配有一个零食分配器。 健康监测器

健康监测器是技术改变我们照顾狗的方式的另一种方式。 这些设备使我们能够实时监控爱犬的健康状况，提供有价值的信息，帮助我们及早发现健康问题。

如果您爱犬的健康状况发生任何变化，PetPace 智能项圈会向您的智能手机发送警报。

另一个值得一提的健康监测器是 FitBark 2。该设备可以跟踪您爱犬的活动水平、睡眠质量和整体健康状况。 该设备带有一个智能手机应用程序，可以让您为您的狗设定健康目标并跟踪它们的进展。 为了扩展您对犬类健康的了解，欢迎您浏览我们的网站。

虚拟培训

虚拟训练是技术改变我们与狗互动方式的另一种方式。 通过虚拟训练，您可以在家中舒适地训练您的爱犬，无需专业训练师。

另一个虚拟训练平台是奋子犬运动学院。 该平台提供各种犬类运动的在线课程，包括服从、拉力和敏捷性。 结论

技术正在以我们从未想象过的方式改变我们与狗互动的方式。 从追踪狗狗活动和行为的智能项圈到帮助我们与宠物交流的应用程序，科技正在改变我们照顾和了解我们毛茸茸的朋友的方式。 无论您是想跟踪您的爱犬的健康状况、逗它们开心还是在家训练它们，都可以使用科技产品或应用程序来帮助您做到这一点。 随着技术的不断发展，我们可以期待未来有更多创新的方式与我们的狗互动。



原文:

For many of us, our dogs are more than just pets - they're beloved members of our families. And with advancements in technology, we're finding new and innovative ways to interact with them. From wearable tech to interactive toys, technology is changing the way we care for and understand our furry friends. In this article, we'll explore the various ways in which technology is transforming our relationship with our dogs.

Smart Collars

One of the most popular tech gadgets for dogs is the smart collar. These collars can track your dog's activity level, location, and even their heart rate. Some smart collars can also monitor your dog's behavior, such as barking, chewing, and scratching, and alert you if there are any changes.

One example of a smart collar is the Whistle GPS Pet Tracker. This collar not only tracks your dog's location, but it also monitors their activity level, and provides real-time updates on their health. The Whistle GPS Pet Tracker is also equipped with Wi-Fi and Bluetooth capabilities, allowing you to monitor your dog's activity even when you're not at home.

Another smart collar worth mentioning is the Link AKC Smart Collar. This collar not only tracks your dog's location and activity level, but it also has a temperature sensor that alerts you if your dog is too hot or too cold. The Link AKC Smart Collar also comes with a smartphone app that allows you to set activity goals for your dog, track their progress, and even store important health information.

Interactive Toys

Interactive toys are another way in which technology is changing the way we interact with our dogs. These toys are designed to stimulate your dog's mind and keep them entertained for longer periods of time.

An interactive toy that showcases the impact of technology on the way we play with our dogs is the PetSafe Automatic Ball Launcher.This toy launches tennis balls for your dog to fetch, providing endless entertainment for your furry friend. The PetSafe Automatic Ball Launcher can be controlled using a remote or a smartphone app, allowing you to launch balls even when you're not at home.

Another popular interactive toy is the iFetch Interactive Ball Launcher. This toy is designed to keep your dog entertained for hours on end, launching tennis balls at different distances and angles. The iFetch Interactive Ball Launcher is also compatible with a smartphone app, allowing you to customize the settings and keep track of your dog's activity.

Communication Devices

Communication devices are another way in which technology is changing the way we interact with our dogs. These devices allow us to communicate with our dogs in ways we never thought possible.

An example of a communication device that demonstrates how technology is changing the way we communicate with our dogs is the PetChatz HD. This device is a video conferencing system that allows you to see and talk to your dog from anywhere in the world. The PetChatz HD also comes with a treat dispenser, allowing you to reward your dog for good behavior even when you're not at home.

Another communication device is the Furbo Dog Camera. This camera allows you to see and talk to your dog from your smartphone, and it also comes with a treat dispenser. The Furbo Dog Camera is equipped with a barking sensor, which sends alerts to your phone when your dog is barking.

Health Monitors

Health monitors are another way in which technology is changing the way we care for our dogs. These devices allow us to monitor our dog's health in real-time, providing valuable information that can help us detect health problems early on.

PetPace Smart Collar is equipped with sensors that monitor your dog's vital signs, including heart rate, respiration, and body temperature. The PetPace Smart Collar sends alerts to your smartphone if there are any changes in your dog's health.

Another health monitor worth mentioning is the FitBark 2. This device tracks your dog's activity level, sleep quality, and overall health. The FitBark 2 is also equipped with Wi-Fi and Bluetooth capabilities, allowing you to monitor your dog's health even when you're not at home. The device comes with a smartphone app that allows you to set health goals for your dog and track their progress. To expand your knowledge about canine health, you're welcome to explore our website.

Virtual Training

Virtual training is another way in which technology is changing the way we interact with our dogs. With virtual training, you can train your dog from the comfort of your own home, without the need for a professional trainer.

Dogo app offers personalized training plans for your dog based on their age, breed, and behavior. The Dogo app also provides video tutorials and step-by-step instructions, making it easy for you to train your dog on your own.

Another virtual training platform is the Fenzi Dog Sports Academy. This platform offers online classes in a variety of dog sports, including obedience, rally, and agility. The Fenzi Dog Sports Academy also offers classes in behavior modification, allowing you to address any behavioral issues your dog may have.

Conclusion

Technology is changing the way we interact with our dogs in ways we never imagined. From smart collars that track our dogs' activity and behavior to apps that help us communicate with our pets, technology is transforming the way we care for and understand our furry friends. Whether you're looking to track your dog's health, keep them entertained, or train them from home, there's a tech gadget or app out there to help you do just that. As technology continues to evolve, we can expect even more innovative ways to interact with our dogs in the future.
item.author: dogkutir

item.title: 如何有效使用自动化 UAT 测试工具 | How to Effectively Use Automated UAT Testing Tools
item.description:
自动化用户验收测试 (UAT) 工具旨在帮助组织简化其软件测试流程并提高其产品的整体质量。

这些工具根据预定义的验收标准自动执行软件测试过程，从而实现更快、更高效的测试。

以下是有效使用自动化 UAT 测试工具的一些技巧：

明确定义验收标准

在开始测试之前，请确保您已经明确定义了软件的验收标准。

这将确保自动化测试准确地检查所需的功能，并确保任何已识别的问题都可以很容易地追溯到验收标准。

使用测试自动化框架

测试自动化框架旨在为自动化测试提供结构化方法。

它们为创建和执行自动化测试提供了一套指南和最佳实践，有助于确保测试可靠且可维护。

创建测试套件

测试套件是一组自动化测试，旨在测试软件的特定方面。

通过为软件的每个方面创建一个测试套件，您可以在对特定区域进行更改时轻松运行测试子集。

使用测试数据生成工具

测试数据生成工具可以帮助自动化为您的自动化测试创建测试数据的过程。

通过使用这些工具，您可以快速生成大量测试数据，这有助于确保您的测试全面准确。

实施持续集成

持续集成 (CI) 是定期将代码更改集成到单个代码库中的做法。

通过自动化集成过程，您可以确保快速识别和解决任何问题。 这可以通过使用像 Opkey 这样的 CI 工具来完成。

监控测试结果

自动化 UAT 测试工具通常会生成详细的测试结果，可用于识别问题和跟踪进度。

请务必定期查看这些结果并采取措施解决发现的任何问题。

把事情简单化

自动化 UAT 测试很快就会变得复杂，尤其是在处理大型复杂的软件系统时。

为避免这种情况，请尽量保持测试简单。 这将使它们更易于维护，也有助于确保它们的可靠性。

结合使用不同的测试方法

自动化 UAT 测试只是测试软件的一种方法。 通过结合使用不同的测试方法，例如手动测试和性能测试，您可以更全面地了解软件并发现更多问题。

结论

总之，自动化 UAT 测试工具对于任何希望提高其软件质量的组织来说都是一项宝贵的资产。

通过遵循上述提示，您可以确保您的自动化测试可靠、可维护且有效。

请记住，自动化测试不是一次性任务，它需要不断努力，以便在软件更改和添加新功能时更新自动化脚本。

与市场上可用的其他商业工具相比，Opkey 是一种经济高效的解决方案。 它的无代码平台可以帮助在几个小时内自动化单个应用程序和跨应用程序测试。 它为用户提供 24*7 的支持和维护，可以帮助您解决使用该工具时可能遇到的任何问题。



原文:


Automated User Acceptance Testing (UAT) tools are designed to help organizations streamline their software testing process and improve the overall quality of their products.

These tools automate the process of testing software against predefined acceptance criteria, allowing for faster and more efficient testing.

Here are some tips for using automated UAT testing tools effectively:

Clearly define acceptance criteria

Before you begin testing, make sure that you have clearly defined the acceptance criteria for the software.

This will ensure that the automated tests are accurately checking for the desired functionality and that any issues that are identified can be easily traced back to the acceptance criteria.

Use test automation frameworks

Test automation frameworks are designed to provide a structured approach to automated testing.

They provide a set of guidelines and best practices for creating and executing automated tests, which can help to ensure that the tests are reliable and maintainable.

Create a test suite

A test suite is a collection of automated tests that are designed to test a specific aspect of the software.

By creating a test suite for each aspect of the software, you can easily run a subset of tests when changes are made to that specific area.

Utilize test data generation tools

Test data generation tools can help to automate the process of creating test data for your automated tests.

By using these tools, you can quickly generate large amounts of test data, which can help to ensure that your tests are comprehensive and accurate.

Implement Continuous Integration

Continuous Integration (CI) is the practice of regularly integrating code changes into a single codebase.

By automating the process of integration, you can ensure that any issues are identified and resolved quickly. This can be done by using a CI tool like Opkey.

Monitor test results

Automated UAT testing tools typically generate detailed test results that can be used to identify issues and track progress.

Be sure to regularly review these results and take action to address any issues that are identified.

Keep it simple

Automated UAT testing can quickly become complex, especially when dealing with large and complex software systems.

To avoid this, try to keep your tests as simple as possible. This will make them easier to maintain and will also help to ensure that they are reliable.

Use a combination of different testing methods

Automated UAT testing is just one method for testing software. By using a combination of different testing methods, such as manual testing and performance testing, you can gain a more complete understanding of the software and identify more issues.

Conclusion

In conclusion, automated UAT testing tools can be a valuable asset for any organization that is looking to improve the quality of their software.

By following the tips outlined above, you can ensure that your automated tests are reliable, maintainable, and effective.

Remember that automating testing is not a one-time task and it requires continuous effort to keep the automation scripts updated as the software changes and new features are added.

Opkey is a cost-effective solution, as compared to other commercial tools available in the market. Its no-code platform can help to automate single app and cross app testing within few hours. It provides 24*7 support and maintenance to the users, which can help you to address any issues you may face while using the tool.
